---
title: "Setup & Infrastructure"
execute:
  eval: false
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

## Setup & Infrastructure

This chapter covers the pieces needed to run the MS project-management app on `mscf-vm`.

## Install Requirements

Clone the repository on the VM and run the requirement checker:

```bash
cd /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management
bash scripts/check_requirements.sh
```

Install missing system or R packages when the checker reports them:

```bash
bash scripts/check_requirements.sh --install
bash scripts/check_requirements.sh --install-r
```

The VM package check includes Shiny, `shinyjs`, DBI, RSQLite, digest, `DT`, rJava, and `mailR`. The app still starts if `DT` or `mailR` is absent, but the VM should install them so searchable tables and email behave like production.

## Install Shiny Server

Install Shiny Server from Posit's `.deb` package:

```bash
sudo apt install -y gdebi-core
wget https://download3.rstudio.org/ubuntu-20.04/x86_64/shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb
sudo gdebi shiny-server-1.5.23.1030-amd64.deb
sudo systemctl enable shiny-server
sudo systemctl start shiny-server
```

## Configure App Folder

```bash
sudo usermod -a -G shiny "$USER"
sudo mkdir -p /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo chmod 755 /srv/shiny-server /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
```

Deploy the app files:

```bash
cd /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management
sudo rsync -av --delete --exclude 'ms_projects.db' --exclude '.Renviron' ms-app/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/
sudo chown -R shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
```

## Initialize SQLite

```bash
cd /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo -u shiny Rscript setup_database.R
```

This creates `ms_projects.db`, the local `admin` user, budget holders, project types, status options, reference organisms, email templates, and the MS-specific project/sample/upload tables.

Use this when you want to check or seed without deleting an existing database:

```bash
cd /srv/shiny-server/ms-app
sudo -u shiny MS_RESET_DB=0 Rscript setup_database.R
```

##  Deploy (VM)

After the database is set, we need to deploy the app and run the texts to check for the settings. This is especially important, if the VM still shows the local login page after the ldap prompt. We need to run `deploy.sh` so `/srv/shiny-server/ms-app/app.R` gets the latest LDAP fixes and updates.

On the VM:

```bash
cd /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management
git pull
./scripts/deploy.sh
```

Before running it, we need to make sure `/srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron` has the [full block](#runtime-environment), because `deploy.sh` preserves `.Renviron` and does not overwrite it:

After deploy, confirm the deployed app contains the new LDAP code:

```bash
sudo grep -n "client_url_search\|auth-status-box\|is_ldap_mode" /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/app.R
```

`deploy.sh` runs LDAP smoke tests and asks for an LDAP username/password unless we set `SKIP_SMOKE_TEST=1`. For a quick deploy without that prompt:

```bash
SKIP_SMOKE_TEST=1 ./scripts/deploy.sh
```

## Runtime Environment

Create `/srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron`:

```bash
sudo tee /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron >/dev/null <<'EOF'
AUTH_MODE=ldap
TRUST_PROXY_AUTH_USER_QUERY=1
LDAP_URI=ldaps://ldapserv1.biochem.mpg.de,ldaps://ldapserv2.biochem.mpg.de,ldaps://ldapserv3.biochem.mpg.de
LDAP_BASE_DN=dc=biochem,dc=mpg,dc=de
LDAP_ATTR_UID=uid
LDAP_ATTR_MAIL=mail
LDAP_ATTR_PHONE=telephoneNumber
LDAP_ATTR_OU=ou
LDAP_ATTR_CN=cn
LDAP_ATTR_GIVENNAME=givenName
LDAP_ATTR_SN=sn
MS_FACILITY_EMAIL=ms-service@biochem.mpg.de
MS_MAIL_FROM=ms-service@biochem.mpg.de
MS_POOL_FALLBACK_EMAIL=omicsdesk@biochem.mpg.de
SMTP_HOST=msx.biochem.mpg.de
SMTP_PORT=25
SMTP_SSL=0
SMTP_TLS=0
# Optional: comma-separated recipient domains, for example biochem.mpg.de.
# MS_EMAIL_ALLOWED_DOMAINS=biochem.mpg.de
MS_UPLOAD_ROOT=/fs/pool/pool-bcfngs-ms-projects
MS_MAX_UPLOAD_MB=75
EOF
sudo chown shiny:shiny /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo chmod 640 /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/.Renviron
sudo systemctl restart shiny-server
```

The upload pool may not exist yet. If it is missing, the app stores uploads under `uploads_pending_pool/` inside the app folder and logs/sends a facility notification instead of blocking project submission.

## Apache LDAP Proxy

Copy the Apache template:

```bash
sudo cp /home/yeroslaviz/BCFMS-project-management/scripts/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-available/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf
```

Enable required modules and the site:

```bash
sudo a2enmod ssl proxy proxy_http proxy_wstunnel headers rewrite ldap authnz_ldap
sudo a2ensite ms-vm-shiny-443.conf
sudo apache2ctl -t
sudo systemctl restart apache2
```

The template proxies `/ms-app/`, protects it with LDAP, and rewrites to `/ms-app/?auth_user=<logged-user>` so Shiny can provision or find the corresponding user row.

## Smoke Tests

```bash
curl -Ik https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/
curl -Ik -u USER https://mscf-vm.biochem.mpg.de/ms-app/
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3838/ms-app/
```

Expected behavior:

- `/` redirects to `/ms-app/`
- unauthenticated HTTPS requests receive an LDAP challenge
- authenticated `/ms-app/` requests redirect once to the canonical `auth_user`
- the Shiny backend answers on port `3838`

## Backups

Before risky maintenance:

```bash
sudo rsync -av /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ /srv/shiny-server/ms-app.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)/
sudo cp /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db /srv/shiny-server/ms-app/ms_projects.db.bak.$(date +%F-%H%M%S)
sudo cp /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/ms-vm-shiny-443.conf.backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M)
```

## External Reference

The older NGS VM documentation remains useful for deeper Apache, certificate, and LDAP troubleshooting. Treat it as background reference, not as an instruction source for app names or paths in this MS repository.
